findings.evidence: New bodies at ProcessShift.lean:58-60 and 75 match Mathlib: `def Stream' (α) := ℕ → α`, `def cons ... | 0 => a | n + 1 => s n`, and `def tail ... := fun i => s.get (i + 1)` in Mathlib/Data/Stream/Defs.lean:24,29-31,42; Mathlib also already has `Stream'.tail_cons` at Data/Stream/Init.lean:48-50.
findings.fix: Define these as pointwise aliases of `Stream'.cons`/`Stream'.tail` or drop the new names and state the measurability/comap lemmas using `fun ω => Stream'.cons (x ω) (t ω)` and `fun ω => Stream'.tail (t ω)`.
findings.issue: `processCons` and `processTail` duplicate Mathlib's `Stream'.cons` and `Stream'.tail` on the same underlying type `ℕ → α`, creating a parallel sequence API.

findings.evidence: The new proof at Cylinder.lean:60-70 uses `Finset.prod_eq_one`/`Finset.prod_eq_zero`; Mathlib has `Set.indicator_pi_one_apply` at Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/GroupWithZero/Finset.lean:48-50, exactly relating a finite pi-set indicator to the product of coordinate indicators.
findings.fix: Rewrite the cylinder as the preimage of `(Set.univ : Set (Fin m)).pi C` under `fun ω i => X (k i) ω` and derive the identity from `Set.indicator_pi_one_apply`, keeping only a thin process-level specialization if needed.
findings.issue: `blockIndicatorProd_eq_indicator` reproves the finite product-of-indicators-as-pi-indicator lemma by a case split instead of reusing Mathlib's existing indicator-pi theorem.

summary: The shifted-path API now records reuse of TauCeti's existing `shift`, but the PR still adds parallel wrappers for stream cons/tail and reproves an existing finite-product indicator identity instead of reusing Mathlib's APIs.
